channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Injured Alex Cobb dropped from Guardians' ALCS roster, replaced by Ben Lively
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Pitcher Alex Cobb was dropped from Cleveland's American League Championship Series roster on Tuesday after he got hurt yet again in the opener, leaving the Guardians in search of a Game 5 starter against the New York Yankees. “It’s been a challenging year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Texas man facing execution in shaken baby syndrome case awaits clemency ruling
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — A Texas man who this week could be the first person in the U.S. executed for a murder conviction tied to the diagnosis of shaken baby syndrome awaited a decision Wednesday on his request for clemency from a state board.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Diddy accuser: He 'had something to do' with Tupac Shakur murder
item.description: Diddy accuser Ashley Parham: Rape was "payback" for saying she believed he "had something to do with the murder" of Tupac Shakur.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans challenge more than 63,000 voters in Georgia, but few removed, AP finds
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — From Georgia's mountains to its Atlantic shore, challenges to the qualifications of voters have rolled in this summer and fall, part of a wide-ranging national effort coordinated by Donald Trump’s allies to enlist Republican activists to remove people they view as suspect from the vot
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY and CHARLOTTE KRAMON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: ISIS-K orchestrated foiled Election Day terror plot: Report
item.description: Afghan native Nasir Ahmad Tawhedi, 27, was arrested in early October and accused of planning to target people on Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: North Carolina man shot, killed outside of his own wedding; family wants answers
item.description: Tyrek Burton, 37, was fatally shot outside his own wedding reception at Barber Park Events Center in Greensboro, North Carolina, over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Elijah Skipper, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bills acquire wide receiver Amari Cooper from Browns, giving QB Josh Allen a much-needed playmaker
item.description: Amari Cooper went from a fading No. 1 wide receiver in Cleveland to a top one with Buffalo. Looking to add a playmaker for quarterback Josh Allen, the Bills acquired Cooper on Tuesday in a trade with the skidding Browns, whose season seems to be unraveling by the week with the NFL’s worst offense.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW and TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Senate Intel chair frets about foreign actors spreading hurricane, election misinformation
item.description: Senate Intelligence Committee Chair Mark Warner (D-Va.) on Tuesday indicated that he is concerned foreign actors are spreading misinformation related to the recovery from a pair of recent hurricanes and will be ramping up efforts amid the final election sprint.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Hundreds of troops kicked out under 'don't ask, don't tell' get upgraded to honorable discharges
item.description: The Pentagon says more than 800 military personnel have seen their service records upgraded to honorable discharges after previously being kicked out of the military under its former “don’t ask, don’t tell” policy.
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Man to row across the Pacific Ocean to honor victims of war in Ukraine
item.description: If he succeeds, 44-year-old Aurimas Mockus from Lithuania will be the first person in the world to complete the 12,000-kilometer (nearly 8,000 mile) trip alone in a rowing boat, without help or stops.
item.dc:creator: Dan Plante, NewsNation

item.title: LSUS partners with Indian institute for dual graduate degree program
item.description: LSU Shreveport chancellor Robert Smith said the campus looks forward to welcoming Indian students into the program.
item.dc:creator: Jaclyn Tripp, NewsNation

item.title: Coca-Cola recalls 'zero sugar' lemonade after it's found to contain full sugar
item.description: The recall, which Coca-Cola issued in September but didn’t announce publicly, applied to 13,152 cases, each with 12 cartons of 12 cans of 12 ounces of lemonade.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: Idaho will begin using deep veins as backup for lethal injection executions, officials say
item.description: Idaho will begin using central veins deep in the groin, neck, chest or arm for executions by lethal injection if attempts to insert standard IV lines fail.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Boone, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ex-husband of 'Real Housewives' star gets seven years for hiring mobster to assault her boyfriend
item.description: The ex-husband of “Real Housewives of New Jersey” cast member Dina Manzo has been sentenced to seven years in prison for hiring a reputed mobster to assault her boyfriend in exchange for the defendant hosting a lavish wedding reception for the attacker.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: FEMA resumes door-to-door visits in North Carolina after threats tied to disinformation
item.description: ASHEVILLE, N.C. (AP) — Federal disaster personnel have resumed door-to-door visits as part of their hurricane-recovery work in North Carolina, an effort temporarily suspended amid threats that prompted officials to condemn the spread of disinformation.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA and SARAH BRUMFIELD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court turns away self-proclaimed Republican’s defense in claiming party’s support
item.description: The Supreme Court turned away a failed Minnesota state Senate candidate’s challenge to rulings that he falsely implied to have the Republican Party’s support during his 2022 write-in campaign.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Can Apple Watch 10 be used as a medical device?
item.description: It's increasingly clear that the future of the Apple Watch isn’t just as an alternative to cell phones.
item.dc:creator: David Lazarus, NewsNation

item.title: Trump rally turns into awkward dance party
item.description: What began as a campaign town hall in the Philadelphia suburbs quickly turned into an impromptu dance party Monday night after Donald Trump was twice interrupted by medical emergencies in the audience.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: Family of mom whose suspected killer is illegal immigrant praises Clinton for calling out Biden-Harris border
item.description: The family of murdered Maryland mom Rachel Morin, whose suspected killer entered the U.S. illegally, is commending former President Bill Clinton for Sunday's border admission.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Alex Murdaugh settles South Carolina lawsuit with Mallory Beach family for 2019 boat crash
item.description: South Carolina Judge Daniel Hall has ordered disgraced attorney-turned-convicted murderer Alex Murdaugh to pay a $500,000 settlement to the family of Mallory Beach.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Husband of Boston nurse Lindsay Clancy, accused of killing their 3 kids, says he wasn’t married to ‘monster’
item.description: Lindsay Clancy allegedly used exercise ropes to strangle and kill her children before attempting suicide. As her trial approaches, her husband Patrick Clancy spoke out in her defense.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jets acquire Raiders wide receiver Davante Adams, AP sources say
item.description: The New York Jets have agreed to terms to acquire disgruntled wide receiver Davante Adams from the Las Vegas Raiders, reuniting him with quarterback Aaron Rodgers, two people with knowledge of the deal told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Knife-wielding woman slashes Virginia police officer before she is shot, bodycam shows
item.description: Virginia police released bodycam footage showing the fatal shooting of Sydney Wilson, 33, after she attacked an officer with a knife.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Petitions for union representation doubled under Biden's presidency, first increase since 1970s
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — There has been a doubling of petitions by workers to have union representation during President Joe Biden's administration, according to figures released Tuesday by the National Labor Relations Board.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Massive meat recall includes hundreds of products sold at Walmart, Target, more
item.description: Check your fridge and freezer: A massive meat recall linked to possible listeria contamination has impacted hundreds of ready-to-eat meals sold at major grocery store chains across the U.S., including Walmart, Trader Joe's and Target.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, KRON4

item.title: Florida returning to something like normal after Hurricane Milton
item.description: ELLENTON, Fla. (AP) — Florida counties hard hit by Hurricane Milton are returning to a semblance of normalcy, with power restored to most areas on Monday, gas stations reopening and students preparing to return to school.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM and CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What to know about shaken baby syndrome as a Texas man could be first in US executed over it
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — A Texas man this week could become the first person executed in the U.S. for a murder conviction tied to the diagnosis of shaken baby syndrome. Robert Roberson, 57, is scheduled to receive a lethal injection on Thursday for the 2002 killing of his 2-year-old daughter, Nikki Curtis.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Members of the Kennedy family gather for funeral of Ethel Kennedy
item.description: CENTERVILLE, Mass. (AP) — Members of the Kennedy family gathered Monday for the funeral of Ethel Kennedy, the wife of the late Sen. Robert F. Kennedy.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: FEMA temporarily pauses aid in parts of NC over reported threats
item.description: The Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) temporarily paused aid in parts of North Carolina this weekend after alleged threats targeted the agency’s personnel aiding in recovery efforts following Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: WATCH: NASA launches Europa Clipper, will scour Jupiter moon for ingredients of life
item.description: Coverage of the launch is expected to begin at 11 a.m. ET and will be added to this story in the video player above. The launch window itself is set to open at 12:06 p.m. ET.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press via Nexstar Media Wire, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Armed man arrested near Trump rally denies 'assassination attempt' claims
item.description: “I’m an artist, I’m the last person that would cause any violence and harm to anybody,” the man has since said.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, Fox 5

item.title: Group associated with polygamous sect builds fences on US Forest Service lands
item.description: A group in Colorado has fenced a parcel of U.S. Forest Service Land in an attempt to claim the land under the Homestead Act of 1862.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, Fox 5

item.title: Influx of homeless encampments under political signages seen across Las Vegas valley
item.description: Seeing political signage on almost every corner is nothing new during an election year, but what’s even more noticeable is the influx of unhoused people making shelter underneath.
item.dc:creator: Madison Kimbro, ABC 4

item.title: Nearly 200 miles of Blue Ridge Parkway reopens as long-term work remains in North Carolina
item.description: The Blue Ridge Parkway was closed indefinitely after Hurricane Helene swept through the region last month.
item.dc:creator: QCN Digital Staff, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What $35 trillion in US national debt means for Americans
item.description: With the national debt standing at more than $35 trillion, economists fear that the country's debt load will only grow.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Deputies 'probably' prevented 3rd Trump assassination attempt, California sheriff says
item.description: A man was arrested outside of a campaign rally for former President Donald Trump in Southern California, and multiple guns were illegally in his possession, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Internet Archive data breach exposes more than 31 million user accounts: reports
item.description: The Internet Archive, a popular digital library known for its Wayback Machine, was hacked and suffered a breach that reportedly exposed the data of 31 million user accounts.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, ABC 4
